news-global-econ.title: Trump should expand his border crackdown. Timing is perfect to rein in Canada and Mexico
news-global-econ.description: The USMCA July deadline gives President Donald Trump leverage to force Canada and Mexico to crack down on fentanyl and illicit trade pipelines.

news-global-econ.title: Ozempic users may be making a major weight-loss mistake, new study suggests
news-global-econ.description: New research finds GLP-1 users significantly reduced physical activity, with daily steps and exercise declining after starting a weight loss medication.

news-global-econ.title: Ex-Vikings captain calls on MLB players to double-down on faith amid Pride Night cap controversy
news-global-econ.description: Former Minnesota Vikings captain Jack Brewer urges Giants pitchers to keep writing Bible verses on Pride Night caps after MLB warning, calling it hypocrisy.

news-global-econ.title: Lines wrap around block as customers wait hours for viral dot cakes that sell out within minutes
news-global-econ.description: A mother-daughter business in Roslyn, New York, created Dot Cakes, the viral rainbow sprinkle desserts now selling out and drawing crowds from other countries.

news-global-econ.title: A problem hiding in plain sight is keeping Americans from buying homes
news-global-econ.description: America's construction industry is short 250,000 workers monthly, extending building timelines and driving up costs amid a 1.5 million-home housing gap.

news-global-soc.title: Environmentalists Warn World Cup ‘Most Polluting Ever’ as FIFA Boss Uses Private Jet to Hit Two Matches a Day
news-global-soc.description: Multiple environmental organizations, activists, and climate scientists have warned that the 2026 FIFA World Cup could potentially be the most polluting sporting event in the history of humanity — a concern that attracted more attention on Tuesday as FIFA con…

news-global-soc.title: FBI helps take down AI phishing ring
news-global-soc.description: The FBI and Google disrupted Outsider Enterprise, a China-based phishing-as-a-service operation tied to 3.87 million stolen credit cards and huge losses.

news-global-soc.title: Trump’s reindustrialization agenda faces its biggest hurdle — Americans
news-global-soc.description: Americans say they want industry back, but local opposition blocks data centers, mines, and factories while China controls critical minerals refining.

news-global-soc.title: Police Rescue Hundreds of Cats from Being Eaten in Vietnam with Bust of Major Animal Theft Ring
news-global-soc.description:
Police in Vietnam seized more than 400 cats in a major bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports.
